content_preview.title: Trump White House Prepares Frontier AI Executive Order as Internal Divisions Slow Response to Mythos Security Threat
content_preview.summary: A coming White House executive order would require AI labs to share frontier models with the government 90 days before public release, but infighting between economic and national security factions has delayed the response to Anthropic's Mythos.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The Trump White House is preparing to release an executive order on AI safety as soon as this week — a striking reversal for an administration that began its term by dismantling Biden-era AI oversight. The order, according to [Axios](https://www.axios.com/2026/05/20/ai-trump-executive-order-white-house-infighting), would establish a voluntary framework under which AI labs share their frontier models with the government at least 90 days before public release. The shift has been force...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt
1 source(s) blocked bot access — sources likely valid but not archivable automatically

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured Analysis piece covering a genuine developing policy story. Logical sections (Overview, What We Know, What We Don't Know, Analysis) are well-executed. The Analysis section provides valuable synthesis without editorializing. Word count (1146) is appropriate for the Analysis category. Writing is clear and professional throughout.
editor_notes.source_verification: Read source-1 (Axios May 13, Archive.org fallback, confirmed): internal divisions, Hassett FDA quote walkback by Wiles and Sacks, CAISI webpage removal, civilian vs national security tug-of-war — all confirmed. Read source-2 (Axios May 5, Archive.org fallback, confirmed): Biden EO revocation Day 1, Vance Paris speech, 50-to-120 Mythos company restriction, supply chain risk designation, CAISI/DeepMind/Microsoft/xAI agreements — all confirmed. Read source-3 (FNN, confirmed): Hassett exact FDA drug quote confirmed, CAISI 40+ evaluations confirmed, Chris Fall role and MITRE background confirmed, $30M CAISI funding confirmed, America First Policy Institute $50-100M recommendation confirmed, FAS $155M recommendation confirmed. Read source-4 (Fortune, confirmed): Ideological reversal framing confirmed, Chris Fall as Energy Department official and MITRE VP confirmed, Congress January 2026 $10M to expand CAISI confirmed. Read source-5 (Platformer, confirmed): Sacks 'doomer industrial complex' and 'regulatory capture strategy based on fearmongering' confirmed, Vance Paris AI Action Summit confirmed, CAISI rebranding 'last June' (= June 2025) confirmed. Source-0 (Axios May 20) returned HTTP 403 with no snapshot — unverifiable. The '90 days before public release' figure appears in both the article summary and Overview lead attributed to this blocked source. No other available snapshot confirms this specific number. Issue 1: The article's Overview contains the phrase 'ability to find and exploit software vulnerabilities with unprecedented speed' in quotation marks. This phrase is a composite paraphrase — Axios (source-2) describes Mythos as able to 'hunt down cybersecurity flaws with extraordinary speed and precision'; FNN (source-3) says 'capable of quickly finding and exploiting decades-old vulnerabilities in widely used software.' The exact quoted phrase does not appear verbatim in any available snapshot. Issue 2: The '90 days' specific figure (in summary and lead) is attributed to source-0 which is unarchivable. Platformer (source-5) is not in the source allowlist but is a credible specialized tech journalism outlet.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All verifiable claims are well-supported: Biden EO revocation Day 1 ✓, Vance Paris summit quote ✓, CAISI 40+ evaluations ✓, Chris Fall background ✓, CAISI $30M funding and 30 staff ✓, Congress $10M January 2026 ✓, America First Policy Institute $50-100M recommendation ✓, FAS $155M proposal ✓, 50-to-120 Mythos company access restriction ✓, Hassett FDA drug quote ✓, Susie Wiles walkback ✓, CAISI rebranded June 2025 ✓. The '90 days' figure cannot be confirmed from available snapshots (source-0 blocked) but is correctly attributed to a specific Axios article. The quoted phrase 'ability to find and exploit software vulnerabilities with unprecedented speed' is a paraphrase placed in quotation marks — a quote-marks violation. No fabricated events or invented specifics detected beyond the paraphrase-in-quotes issue.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. The Analysis section synthesizes the pattern without adopting advocacy language. The article accurately represents both sides of the internal administration debate. No loaded language or AI self-reference.
editor_notes.originality: Not a duplicate. A related article ('CAISI Signs Frontier AI Testing Agreements', May 6) covered the CAISI announcement specifically. This article provides a distinct analytical synthesis: the White House EO preparation, the internal divisions, the CAISI resource constraints, and the ideological reversal — angles not covered in the prior article. Substantively new.
concerns: Paraphrase inside quotation marks in the Overview: 'ability to find and exploit software vulnerabilities with unprecedented speed' — this phrase does not appear verbatim in any source snapshot. Sources say 'hunt down cybersecurity flaws with extraordinary speed and precision' (Axios) and 'finding and exploiting decades-old vulnerabilities' (FNN). The exact quoted wording is a composite paraphrase.

The '90 days before public release' figure is in both the article summary and Overview lead but is only attributed to source-0 (Axios May 20, HTTP 403, no snapshot available). No other source in the article confirms this specific number. The claim may be accurate but is unverifiable from the provenance record.
Platformer (source-5) is not in the source allowlist. However, Platformer is Casey Newton's well-regarded tech journalism newsletter and directly covered this story.
recommendations: File a corrections note documenting the paraphrase-in-quotes issue for reader transparency.
File a corrections note on the '90 days' figure as an unverifiable specific from a blocked source.
Consider adding platformer.news to the source allowlist.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Substantively strong Analysis piece on a significant developing policy story. All major claims except one are confirmed from available snapshots. Two recoverable issues: a paraphrase placed inside quotation marks in the Overview, and a specific number ('90 days') in the summary/lead that originates from a blocked source with no archive snapshot. Both issues are honest and correctable without undermining the article's substance. APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS.
